items.title: We're sinking deeper: Iranians brace for infrastructure strikes as Trump deadline nears
items.description: Ordinary Iranians respond to the US president's threat to destroy Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say
items.description: The Israeli strikes reportedly targeted Hamas security personnel after they clashed with an Israeli-backed militia near Maghazi camp.

items.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
items.description: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

items.title: Really feeling the love: Savannah Guthrie returns to NBC as search for mother goes on
items.description: Nancy Guthrie disappeared from her home in Tucson, Arizona, in what authorities believe was an abduction.

items.title: Kanye West's right to enter UK under review after festival outcry
items.description: The rapper's appearance in London in July has caused controversy because of his past antisemitic comments.

items.title: Trump endorses ex-UK political aide Steve Hilton for California governor
items.description: Steve Hilton, who advised the former prime minster and hosted a Fox News show, is running as a Republican.

items.title: Ladysmith Black Mambazo's wise elder dies aged 77
items.description: Albert Mazibuko sang in the iconic South African choral group for more than 55 years.

items.title: US Supreme Court paves way for dismissal of Steve Bannon conviction
items.description: The order allows a lower court to consider dismissing the former Trump strategist's indictment.

items.title: Seven Eritrean players fail to return home after international match
items.description: Several footballers have absconded in the past leaving behind what rights groups call a repressive government.

items.title: Emergency jabs after 100 children die of suspected measles in a month in Bangladesh
items.description: More than 100 people, mostly children, have been killed by measles since mid-March, officials suspect.

items.title: Emergency jabs after 100 children die of suspected measles in a month in Bangladesh
items.description: More than 100 people, mostly children, have been killed by measles since mid-March, officials suspect.

items.title: How China fell for a lobster: What an AI assistant tells us about Beijing's ambition
items.description: The AI agent sparked a frenzy of raising lobsters in March, with users training the tool to suit their needs.

items.title: Two protests, two elections: How Nepal's Gen Z succeeded where Bangladesh's stumbled
items.description: How did a youth movement triumph in one country but fall so short in another?

items.title: You can hire house help in 15 minutes in India - is it fair?
items.description: Instant house help brings speed and structure, but workers face tighter control and rising pressure in their jobs.

items.title: The man who became one of India's greatest stage queens
items.description: Chapal Bhaduri was Bengal's biggest stage queen - until women arrived and the spotlight moved on.

items.title: Unanswered questions remain after Australia's most wanted fugitive killed in standoff
items.description: Double-murderer Dezi Freeman evaded capture for seven months in the bush but police believe he had help.

items.title: How Bombay went from a fort city to a bustling metropolis
items.description: A new exhibition charts Bombay's passage through the centuries via paintings, photographs and prints.

items.title: Watch: Who is the coup leader who will be Myanmar's next president?
items.description: Min Aung Hlaing presided over his last military parade in Myanmar before he takes up the role of president.

items.title: Fears cost of water and beer to soar as India's scorching summer hits
items.description: Makers of plastic and glass bottles are struggling to access raw materials as the war squeezes supplies.

items.title: Iconic 19th Century painting sets Indian art record with $17.9m sale
items.description: Yashoda and Krishna, painted by Raja Ravi Varma, beat a record set by an MF Husain work last year.

items.title: Benefits and pensions rise as two-child cap ends
items.description: Families on some benefits with three or more children will get an average rise of £4,100 a year.

items.title: Don't put off treatment during doctors' strike, NHS tells patients
items.description: The strike comes at the end of the bank holiday weekend and NHS managers fear demand could be challenging.

items.title: Wireless Festival boss defends Kanye West appearance
items.description: Its managing director calls the rapper's past comments abhorrent but asks people to offer some forgiveness.

items.title: Northern Ireland becomes first in UK to give parents two weeks paid leave for miscarriage
items.description: Northern Ireland becomes first part of UK to bring in legal entitlement for parents affected by miscarriage at any stage of a pregnancy to have paid leave.

items.title: Families battle to reclaim thousands of pounds owed by care operator investigated by BBC
items.description: Relatives say it has taken months, and in some cases years, to get back money owed by a care home operator.

items.title: Warmest weather of the year expected with 24C this week
items.description: The warmest weather of the year is expected on Tuesday and Wednesday as temperatures rise above average.

items.title: Couple in court after boy, 13, dies in crash
items.description: A man and a woman are remanded custody in connection with a crash involving a car, a bike and a scooter.

items.title: Hundreds take part in Easter coal-carrying race
items.description: The annual sack-carrying challenge in Gawthorpe began more than 60 years ago.

items.title: I learned Korean to understand K-Pop
items.description: Katy Richards is one of a growing number of fans of K-Pop and Korean culture in the UK.

items.title: Could saunas become the new pub when it comes to socialising?
items.description: Wales' picturesque landscape lends itself to sauna use, in a similar way to Finland, a business owner said.

items.title: トランプ大統領 軍と共同で記者会見【ノーカット動画】
items.description: アメリカのトランプ大統領は6日午後、ホワイトハウスで記者会見を行いました。

items.title: 高市首相 イラン大統領との電話会談を行う方向で調整
items.description: 緊迫する中東情勢をめぐり、高市総理大臣は、イランのペゼシュキアン大統領との電話会談を行う方向で調整しており、事態の早期沈静化やホルムズ海峡の航行の安全確保などを直接働きかけたい考えです。

items.title: トランプ氏“期限過ぎれば発電所など失う”イラン側は停戦拒否
items.description: アメリカのトランプ大統領は、6日の記者会見で、イランのエネルギー施設への攻撃を行わない期限について、アメリカ東部時間の7日午後8時、日本時間の8日午前9時だと改めて強調し、期限を過ぎればイランは発電所などを失うことになると警告しました。

items.title: “イランがアメリカに停戦拒否の回答” イラン国営通信
items.description: イランの国営通信は6日、アメリカとの協議をめぐって、イランが仲介国のパキスタンを通じてアメリカに回答を送り、停戦を拒否するとともに戦闘の恒久的な終結が必要だと強調したと伝えました。

items.title: 今年度予算案 きょう採決が行われれば成立する公算大きく
items.description: 今年度予算案は、7日、参議院予算委員会で高市総理大臣も出席して集中審議が行われたあと、締めくくりの質疑が行われます。

items.title: 航空会社の多く 国内線で運賃値上げや燃油サーチャージ検討
items.description: イラン情勢を受けて原油価格が高騰する中、NHKが国内の航空会社6社を取材したところ、多くの社が国内線について、運賃の値上げや、現在ほとんど適用する会社のない燃油サーチャージの導入を検討していることが分かりました。

items.title: 観光地 イタリア・ベネチア 日帰り観光客に入場料の徴収開始
items.description: 「水の都」として知られる世界有数の観光地、イタリアのベネチアでは日帰りの観光客を対象にした入場料の徴収がことしも今月から始まりました。
